how_to_approve: Review every rule and rationale. To approve, re-run with approvals={"rules": {"approver": "<your name>", "sha256": "7a6b6c3cb955862d3254b94f2931f6eba600a05ed397710d0bd362145e687df8"}}. The hash binds the approval to exactly this draft.
rationales: 'id' has zero nulls across 2,000 rows - the profile suggests completeness is an expectation, so future nulls should be exceptions.
'id' is distinct on 2,000 of 2,000 rows (ratio >= 0.999) - it behaves as an identifier, so duplicates should be exceptions.

'date' is distinct on 2,000 of 2,000 rows (ratio >= 0.999) - it behaves as an identifier, so duplicates should be exceptions.
'date' is a TIMESTAMP column - future-dated records are a validity finding in most operational data.
